text:
The issue with the AI productivity argument is that we're not all meant to be productive at the levels capitalism demands of us. And those who are, well… we're only meant to be productive around what we're excited about.

The reality is, only about 70% of the population is designed to have sustainable, consistent energy for regular work, which means that roughly 2.5 billion people aren't.

That's a lot of people.

Since capitalism rewards productivity and wrongly attributes less of it to personal or moral failings, many will interpret that data through their conditioning.

They'll...

😔 Assume the 70% is "better" and that something is wrong with them if they're not wired for consistent productivity.

😇 Dismiss me because they're good at being productive and therefore buy into the lie that they're somehow "better."

All I'm saying is that the goal of being more productive is flawed in more ways than one. If you buy into the idea that you should use AI because it will make you more productive (and you feel that urgency to "keep pace" with other people), not only will you burn yourself out, but you'll further buy into a system that's intentionally designed to exploit your labor and FOMO so they (the people who created the systems) can make more money.

Even if you're part of the 70%, you'll likely still burn out, as many already have, because producing just to remain the loudest person in a deafening space means you're not likely following your intuitive 'yes'. You're just playing the game.

If you're going to focus on being "more productive," why not focus those efforts on something that will gain more reach, visibility, and momentum ✨ the longer it's live ✨ (unlike social media content that vanishes into the noise within seconds to minutes to maaaybe hours)?

That's exactly what we're covering in our upcoming workshop: Creating a Content Ecosystem That Thrives Beyond the AI Bubble.

Learn more and grab your ticket:
https://channelsforchange.tv/ecosystem/
bert_topic_keywords: ai, stocks, stock, artificial intelligence, artificial, intelligence, market, nvidia, investors, stock market

text:
I was really hurt and shocked that my opponent authorized a mailer which hit me with multiple false points.  I have run a very positive campaign and I will continue to run a positive campaign.

The mailer alleged that I am cozying up to far right extremists... which could not be more false. I stood strong against Wendy Rogers. I helped run the campaign to replace John Fillmore. I get death threats nearly every day from far-right extremists who want to get rid of Jewish people. I will ALWAYS stand strong against the far right.

The hit piece also alleged that I defended an abortion ban and election denialism... which couldn't be more false. I called to REPEAL the 1870s abortion ban years before it was finally repealed and I have always said that abortion is an issue which the voters should decide on the ballot. I'm really confused as to where my opponent got the idea that I'm into election denialism. Joe Biden is the President. Katie Hobbs is our Governor.

I got involved in politics because I was upset with the direction of both political parties and I wanted to be part of the change I wish to see. My grandparents are Democrats in our district, I'm a Republican. We can agree to disagree on certain issues peacefully. One of my best friends was a Democratic legislator this past year. I fully embrace working across the aisle to do what is right for ALL Arizonans and I am completely opposed to any far right extremists who want to turn America into a theocracy.

I will not go low on my opponents. I am the only Arizonan in this race and I know that Arizonans want positive campaigns about what we should be doing for the people of our state - not partisan nonsense which spreads more negativity into an already vitriolic political climate. I reached out to Stephanie Simacek just as King Priam reached out to Achilles in the Iliad to ask that even opponents can respect one another.

I'm just a guy who grew up here and wants my kids to be able to grow up here. I want well-funded schools, long-term water security, and an economy that puts more money into your pocket.

If you have any questions, please reach out to me. My email is aribradshawaz@gmail.com and I'll respond to everything I can.

Politics isn't about smears. It's about doing the right thing for our community. I'll always put our community over partisan politics.

Thank you so much for reading this. My name is Ari Bradshaw, and I hope to earn one of your two votes for State House.

text:
Stream of Consciousness ...

OREGON Homelessness Solved!

■ PLEASE CONTACT GOVERNOR KOTEK & YOUR CONGRESSIONAL DISTRICT REPRESENTATIVES!

GOV KOTEK (503) 378-4582
(800) 237-3710 (In State Only)
(503) 947-1394 (Direct)
FAX (503) 947-1472
TTY: 7-1-1

900 Court Street
Suite 254
Salem, Oregon 97301-4047

EMAIL: governor.kotek@oregon.gov

Reference
Homelessness Initiative Proposal by
Steve William Laible, MBA
Retired Mustang (Captain/USAF)
Oregon Native Son (Ashland)

Recent Candidate for Congress (CD2)

#Keynote #Listener

▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎☆☆☆☆☆▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎

■ Project Camp Tenderheart Initiative
(Big Top Circus Tents) Eastern Oregon (aka) Idaho

■ TELL THE GOVERNOR YOU AGREE WITH THIS PLAN TO RESOLVE OREGON'S HOMELESSNESS CRISIS, ONCE AND FOR ALL!

■ TELL THE GOVERNOR WE MUST TAKE BACK OUR DOWNTOWNS OF OUR HOMETOWNS!

■ Modify or Suggest improvements by adding your own ideas to help the Camp Tenderheart initiative.

▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎☆☆☆☆☆▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎▪︎

-----  <governor.kotek@oregon.gov>; Rep.PamMarsh@oregonlegislature.gov <rep.pammarsh@oregonlegislature.gov>
Sent: Tuesday, February 25, 2025 at 09:32:26 PM PST
Cc: #Oregonian #USAToday #WallStreetJournal #WashingtonPost #NYTimes #DailyCourier #RegisterGuard #BendBulletin #SenWyden #SenMerkley 

Subject: Homelessness

Hello.

Dear Governor & Representative Marsh,

     I've been thinking a lot about this. I even devoted some ideas in my book, that do not include these new ones, so I thought I would take the liberty here, with you both, now.

(Reference the BELOW the fold article, this date, in the Josephine County, Grants Pass, Daily Courier:  "Kotek asks Legislature to create statewide homeless shelter system at a cost of $218M.")

     (Though, I believe you've actually budgeted $2B+ and here we are, nowhere. Square one. Ground zero.)

     Let me save you a nickel.

     Obviously (and arguably) we don't have a homelessness crisis, we have an addiction crisis.

     I'm sure you will both agree, short of that, we still have a humanitarian issue that demands empathy and grace, and ACTION!

     I don't believe government can solve the problem facing them, given the political countermeasures, undermining, public pressure, and backlash that comes with hot topics. Let alone, the conflicts of interests, funding and influence.

     It's going to take real outside-of-the box thinking. And seriously, when was the last time a body of three ever agreed on anything, unanimously?

     I have the enthusiasm and problem solving gene. (For I am man!) 😁

     I have addressed similar issues or crises in my book, America the Beautiful.

     Reverse Migration, for instance.

     So why not adopt a similar paradigm thesis with this wholly inescapble, on-going homelessness issue?

    Whether you identify as a round or square peg, we need to recognize, neither fits on the board game of life!

     We cannot coexist, nor should we, as a civilized society living in harmony, and a homeless one, living within our boundaries, excused from all taxes and responsibilties, yet benefitting from many public services...

     I believe it's a choice to be broken!

    ● Taxpayers, voters, deserve better! And quite frankly, so do the police, and oddly enough, the homeless population.

     ■ First up, the "Secret Sauce." We need to incentivize the homeless population with their forever homes.

     Oregon can be the leader!

     Oregon can devise a successful pilot program for the country to emulate.

     The second task is to triage the homeless population. Assessing the greatest needs and priorities, and addressing that.

                                     ***

     I envision a homeless population living freely amongst themselves, without daily roustings and harassment, in unforced and ungated communities, known perhaps, as "Nature Heights" or better yet, "Camp Tenderheart."

     Where we allow them to regulate themselves, free of community practices and standards, and ordinances, miles from our parks & streams, our downtowns of our hometowns!

    The collective and cumulative effects will be astounding. Communities will save mega millions on all that goes with homelessness.

     To wit: enforcement, including legal suits, appeals #SCOTUS trips through the ineffective revolving court system, which includes jail, ungodly encampment clean-ups of horrific filthy garbage disasters they call home; hospital visits, and sleepovers at sobering centers.

     Each takes a toll on our law enforcement personnel, fire station crews, doctors, nurses, admitting staff, ambulance medics, jailers, judges, juries, business owners, and the rest of us.

     All at the cost of law-abiding taxpayers, who have lost their parks, sense of safety, beautiful and scenic vistas. The children suffer because colorblind adults can't or won't solve the Rubik cube!

     Once the homeless population exits our fair cities, peace and tranquility will fill the daily morning sunrises, as does the morning dew, and will refresh and enliven our communities.

     Gone are the eyesores that are the unsightly garbage encampments, in our neighborhoods, parks, business districts, alleyways, along our lone corridor freeway, roadways, and under bridges, etc.

     Gone is the defecating, urinating, vomiting, and fornicating, human beings, in our public domains.

     Shopkeepers know this, first-hand. Ask them how many times their storefronts have been vandalized, requiring a hose and water to remove the excrement bad behavior.

     FOR THE LOVE OF MIKE,
     WE MUST DO BETTER!!!

     (We're the "civilized" ones, right!?)

      We cannot allow or tolerate this "crappy" behavior a moment more!  

     STOP KICKING THE CAN, ALREADY!

    Safety will be restored, to a GREAT degree. The majority of early morning car break-ins, prowling, shoplifting, gun or knife play, 911 calls and dispatches of police and ambulances, will be vanquished from our repeat offenders, the homeless!

     Law enforcement ought not ever be asked to clean another garbage dump, complete with syringes, feces, vomit, etc. (Besides, don't we have inmates for such tasks?)

     But I digress.

     I see a place where they (homeless) can live in peace. Where stability exists.

     Now, I'm just spitballing here, but what if "we" could secure some ideal parcels of land, throughout the state, far from society. Perhaps, at least 25 miles. 200 would be better.

     We would build a safe, livable, sustainable, habitat for humanity, for those desperate souls, who call the stars and the great outdoors their home.

     A place worthy of our deployed veterans, where tents are home, latrines, with hot running water for showers and laundry are present. Where chow is wolfed down before sirens are played on loudspeakers, alerting of incoming rockets. So before you whine, bitch or otherwise complain, think about that!

     Where housing is available. Not only available housing, but enough to house the entire Oregon homeless population!!

     Where communities-at-large would show their grace with an abundance of physical item donations, including goods and services and cash!

     Where an abundance of clothing is possible. A canteen (on site store) bursting with supplies. And yes, where wine & beer are on-hand, and some California grass is available, too!

    ●  There's another great #Incentive to relocate, right there!

     ■ I can see a repurposing for those gigantic main show BIG TOP circus tents, which could serve as an outer shell for dry shelter warmth and cooling, as well as hiding encampment eyesores. Depending on the season/temperatures. Yes, seasonal in nature, given the weight of snowfall. But at least 8+ months of use, annually.

    ● The largest of these Big Top circus tents can seat 9K people in their 74K square footage, of living space!

     (Lions, tigers, and elephants, not included.)

     ■ Inside the Big Top tents, we can install those massive Major League Baseball (MLB) infield tarps, keeping separation from the ground and residents. Moreover, we can install plywood sidewalks for those confined to wheelchairs and scooters. 

    ● Shipping pods used in transporting household goods, local and out of state, could be arranged in cul-de-sac fashion. These are basic pods. Cost effective fiberglass shells with plywood flooring. But easily, and more importantly, better than pup tents.

     ●● Each pod could be furnished with a bed or an air mattress, locking footlocker to store/secure residents' personal items of value. Each pod could have an approved portable fire pit, with a screened dome cover.

     ● Larger shipping containers could be repurposed and dedicated, holding all manner of supplies and essentials, to include the Canteen (store) itself, with cigarettes, cigars, tobacco, clothing, coats, sweaters, scarves, food, snacks, chips, crackers, licorice, Snickers, sodas, cookies, candy, socks, shoes, apples, fruits, vegetables, soap, even bubble bath, deodorant, toiletpaper, shampoo, tooth brushes, toothpaste, mouthwash, Q tips, laundry detergents, mirrors, towels, wash cloths, nail clippers, backpacks, tools, lumber, hammers, nails, screws, levels, hand-saws, work gloves, shovels, rope, twine, pet food and pet supplies, etc.

     ■ Social programs really ought to be more like trampolines, the least we can do is provide hammocks.

     ● Moreover, a refurbished shipping container could become a bowling alley, others can house pool tables, basketball hoops, and ping pong tables could also find their way inside the recreational shipping containers.

   ■ Goodwill & Salvation Army items could fill another shipping container, from all the apparel the homeless could ever use or want, from winter gloves, mittens, boots, umbrellas, blankets, sheets, pillows (another luxury item incentive) pants, dry socks, bras, underwear, shelving, racks, tables, including decks of cards & card tables, board games, hats, sweaters, sweatshirts, to balls & bats (scoccer balls/nets, footballs, basketballs, golf balls, baseballs, softballs), badminton rackets/nets, disc golf, Frisbees, pickleballs & paddles, hula hoops, tetherballs, marbles, and chalk for hopscotch, etc.

    ● Yes, huge lots & crates of "gifts" from appreciative communities with big hearts, will sustain these camps.

     (Camp Tenderheart's 1, 2, 3 ...)

     It's a Win-Win!

     ● An on-site library, is essential. As are a million other things, I've not yet thought of here, in my initial Stream of Consciousness ...

     ● No firearms, and no children shall be allowed to live at any of the Camp Tenderheart's. 

     ● They can also bury their dead in their own graveyard, just as native Americans and new frontier pioneers did. 

     ● I suppose after a trial run with lessons learned, you could develop a dozen or so like camps throughout the state.

     ■ Mainly or mostly, these people DO NOT fit in with 'our' societal norms. They can't or won't follow our rules. So let's help them achieve the nomadic lifestyle they so richly desire and desperately deserve. 

     ■ Governor, you've earmarked $218M in your latest legislative mandate initiative to house the homeless. This letter is but a 'seed' for your legislative body to ignore, argue about, find flaws with, poke holes in, because quite frankly, that's what they do.

     ● So maybe, remove them from the equation! Sign one of those ever-popular, in vogue, Executive Action letters (orders) and privatize this one particular calamity.

     ■ My Disneylandesque recommendation will benefit and finally solve the unhoused homeless population, as no other measure or idea can.

     ● Nourish the vision "seed" with encouragement and support, and watch the fields of lilies bloom.

     •• This will be FUN, a great adventure!

     ● Of course, a system of checks and balances are required. As is a hand-up track, for those looking to better their station in life. 

     ■ Ongoing communication is essential. As is healthcare, doctors, dentists, but the homeless have rejected these civilized societal perks. Maybe we can arrive at some 'Scouting of America' compromises. First-aid kits, pliers for dentistry, and elixirs. I'm certainly not suggesting a helipad or dentists and doctors within borders. 

     ● Surely, there is still some humanity left out there. Faith based organizations, while well-meaning, have mostly been rejected by this class. So, they MUST receive an invitation by Camp Elders yo visit. They cannot simply show up, unannounced, and begin witnessing.

     ■ The homeless need to make their own rules. The rules that work for them! We need to respect that. Let's remember how happy and content they were, portrayed in One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest. They truly want to be left alone, until they don't.

     ● Obviously, the mentally ill, imparied, challenged, are NOT good candidates for Camp Tenderheart.

     Nor are sexual predators, convicts, felons, or those nondesirables, their own shun.

     ● Camp Tenderheart is meant to emulate is a 60s hippie commune.

    •• With tall stalks of fresh growing corn, free-range chickens, goats, pigs, cows, etc. Better to live off of the land, than panhandling, on the steps of post offices or store entrances, right?

     ● Ongoing education is not feasible. These bodies are a glide path of their own design, their own choosing. The best we can offer is our respect and some level palliative, holistic care. 

     ● Think of them as pioneers, as my great grandmother was, who traveled the mighty Oregon Trail from Missouri, in a covered wagon. Even my late mother used an outhouse in her youth.

     ■ The homeless don't require the same living standards as in civilized society. Best you recognize the differences. We DO NOT owe them FREE HOUSES, no matter how tiny. They DO NOT belong within our boundaries.

    ■ We are living in an entitle generation, as it is. We all take so much for granted. Maybe we can reconcile letting those who wish to dance to a different drummer's beat, do so. It's the right thing to do.

     ■ I would bet my last $100 black Seven Feathers casino chip, a new awakening is coming. Giving our homeless the "gift" of humanity, in an effective, efficient manner, matters.

     ■ Naturally, the smart thing is to bring the homeless to the table. Let's see what they think. What they want. What will make them happy.

     ● What will give us back our communities.

    ■ Let's go ahead and lean in. Let's do whatever it takes. Let's be willing pay the ransom.

    ■ And while we're helping them improve their quality of life, the "intended" consequence is, we're doing the same for ours.

     ■ Modern conveniences are really meant for those willing to live within the norms, right? It's a cherished entitlement.

     ■ Turn your back on civilized society, laws, norms, drink yourself to death, and you best bet modern day advancements will turn their backs on you. For life comes with no guarantees. You are not entitled to be given a free ride in life. Let alone, a free tiny home! That's politics!!

     ■ So let's go BIG! BIG TOP CIRCUS TENT BIG... sprinkled with cost effective efficient pods.

     ● With one exception: Those lost souls incapable of taking care of themselves, however they arrived at this point, need us. As a civilized society, we have a duty, an honor code to help.

     ■ Mostly, this Camp, or "reservation" of sorts, must be clear from forests & streams, for obvious reasons. Pollution to our enviornment, is an real and present danger, especially, with this grouping of tribal nomads.

     ■ We can secure and close the deal with our last "incentive" for "our" homeless population, to move to Camp Tenderheart, by "gifting" each resident a stipend of some amount. Some agreeable monetary value, say $100 a month!  For some yet to be determined period, based on how well this experiment goes.

     ● It will total way less than the hundreds of millions of dollars already spent, aka wasted.

     ■ Again, incentivize, incentivize, incentivize! Problem solved.

     ● This money can be used to buy luxury items in their Camp Tenderheart community canteen. Everything can be priced at $1. Sure, they will shoplift, but maybe not, if it's their community store.

     •• They will police themselves, with consequences & inintended consequences.

    ●  Most, if not all homeless, are skilled at something in their lifetimes. Camp leaders will come forward. They will emerge.

     ● Their community might surprise you. They may do more, with less. They may do more than just exist. They may actually surprise you.

     ● They may even thrive! Before they earn their 🪽 wings 🪽...

    ■ Governor, "If you ALWAYS do, as you've ALWAYS done, you'll ALWAYS get, what you've ALWAYS got!

     ● Make sense?

Thank you for the privilege of your time.

■ POST SCRIPT

To: governor.kotek@oregon.gov <governor.kotek@oregon.gov>; rep.pammarsh@oregonlegislature.gov <rep.pammarsh@oregonlegislature.gov>
Sent: Thursday, February 27, 2025 at 08:15:15 AM PST
Cc: #Oregonian #USAToday #WallStreetJournal #WashingtonPost #NYTimes #DailyCourier #RegisterGuard #BendBulletin #SenWyden #SenMerkley 

Subject: HOMELESS P. S. CLARIFICATION

Hello. I apologize for the post script but I needed to clarify the proposed $100 stipend.

     ■ I am NOT an advocate for the homeless!

    ■  I AM an advocate for decency. For the rule of law, for police who have a disgusting job description when tasked to clean-up homeless encampments, over and over again. For our children who must tolerate homelessness in their communities, because adults are wholly incompetent and impotent. Who must deal with the worst of us. For humanity. For doing the right thing. For addressing ignorance. For finding measurable, sustainable solutions. For government to be effective and efficient.

     ● Kindness Wins the Day!

    ● For children.

     ● The Global Kindness Initiative. Civility. Common sense. Education. For integrity. For truth, justice and the American way. For fellowship, neighborship and lending assistance wherever needed.

     ■ During my military career, before Congress closed my Air Force base and sent me to the Republic of Korea, on a 16-month remote tour of duty, as the first Operations Support Squadron Section Commander for two F-16 Fighter Squadrons, I designed and built a 16-flavor ice cream store, The Polar Bar, in a California mall, food court.

     ● I created Polar Bucks which is an idea that could be used for Camp Tenderheart where the (no longer homeless) can use to purchase items from their Camp Tenderheart canteen.

     ● This could be a reoccurring monthly disbursement to each resident. $100 or $500, doesn't matter. It only has value in Camp.

    ● If it were decided to fund Camp Tenderheart with real US currency, say for example, $100 x 22k Camp Tenderheart residents, that's a little misleading as an on-going line item expense for taxpayers, inasmuch as that monthly disbursement is actually used to purchase items in the canteen, and then doled back out to camp residents, on the first of each month.

    ● In other words, it's the same recycled money. The State will not be on the hook of an ever-increasing budget for this program.

    ■ By creating a [Dollar Store] format for the canteen, Camp residents will enjoy a sense of normalcy. They will be able to open their donated purses and wallets to buy toilet paper for a buck. Another dollar for a beer or a candy bar, or a new pair of socks.

     ● At the end of the month, the collected canteen money will be redisbursed to residents for the next month's purchases.

     ● It will be, however, quite problematic using real money, without security managers or measures. You really can't have millions of dollars floating around Camp Tenderheart.

                                        ***

     ■ Look, it's flawed inside-the-box logic, (thinking) that you can spend $2B+ to integrate or assimilate the homeless population into society.

     ● That's just NOT realistic!!!!!

    ■ If your goal is to help the homeless population, you must get out of your own way.

     ■ The effective, sustainable solution for the majority of the homeless, is to create a safe space where they can live in peace.

     ● This includes those living in their vehicles.

    ■ So why aren't we creating regions within the state where the entire homeless population can live within their own soceity!?

     ■ Easter Oregon has the largest unpopulated land masses, so why not here?

     ● Besides, if you're a visionary like myself, if Idaho forces get their way, and move the line, Camp Tenderheart goes with it. Now it's Idaho's problem. 😉

     ■ Most choose to be homeless. They do not share your reasoned decision making or common sense. They only want the basics. To have food, drugs, alcohol, warmth, to be dry, safe. Most are content.

     ● Your not listening.
     ● You're judging!

    ■ You ought NOT put the public or landlord's in this position. Buying or renting space in downtown motels is not a long-trem or practical solution. It only solves (temporarily) being unhoused.

     ■ The ADDICTED homeless still DO NOT belong in a civilized society. Taking over our parks, our downtowns. They are incapable and unwilling of compliance. You must accept this reality. 

     ■ The trash they collect, the filth, the lack of ambition, the danger they present to the law abiding public simply cannot be ignored!

     ■ So why are you!?

    ● I'm not talking about a mom with three small children who lost her rental home because her husband lost his job or died. Or she lost her federal job, or her car broke down, or was driven away in the dead if night, by the Repo man. Or that she lost her two jobs and childcare.

     ■ We most certainly should help her and those who find themselves in this circumstance! They ALL will get the help they need, MUCH MUCH quicker.

     ● Hence my initial reference to triage!

     ■ I'm instead talking about those who have given up. Turned their lives over to drugs and alcohol. They no longer work or care to live within the law, rules or normalcy of the life we choose. Hell, most times, they can't even stand up!

     ■ We are different. We will never coexist!

     ● Stop trying to force square pegs into round holes. It's really "that" simple!

     ■ Like the NY city public school system does, in naming their schools, PS 124, PS 189, PS 215, etc., we can adopt a similar numbering system for multiple Camp Tenderhearts throughout the state.

     ■ Maybe we don't resurrect just the largest Big Top circus tents with 74,000 interior square feet, which can seat 9,000 people, but rather incorporate a mix of sizes throughout designated locations, as suitable shelter.

     ● It's way more than native Americans had.

     ● Maybe we use isolated state or federal lands to establish multiple Camp Tenderhearts. 

   ■ Camp Tenderheart locations throughout the state, can each have a separate and unique mission or designation. Each with an eye on scaling the rungs of the ladder, learning what it takes to eventually escape from homelessness, where people can then reentered society as a full, abled body citizen, who will contribute to society as an asset, not a liability.

     ● Unfortunately, most homeless will never escape their lot in life because of deadly addictions.

     ● This is the station in life they chose!

    ● Respect that!

     ■ It's far better for society-at-large if they sit around Camp Tenderheart all day, playing ping pong, attending to chickens, or sleeping off yet another hangover, rather than driving on our streets without insurance, under the influence. Or jumping into traffic, putting our citizens at risk. Or shoplifting on the regular. Or being hauled off to jail for the 72nd time!

    ■ Or the daily roustings by police, having to breakdown their camps, tents, daily, only to wander the downtowns of our hometowns, yet again, 365 days a year; year after year, while politicians rub their hands together looking for the Holy Grail, wasting taxpayer's dollars.

   ■ Imagine that life for yourself. Homeless in Grants Pass, Oregon are rousted every single morning, with their entire worldly possessions in tow, until they are allowed to return to their designated overnight city locations.

     ● That my friend, is the very definition of INSANITY!

     ■ The costs to cities, counties and the state, dealing with homelessness, is ridiculously unmanageable. It's breaking budgets. It's neglecting and abusive to the typical functions of government.

     ■ Leadership on this inhumanity crisis is failing miserably! Those in suits and ties with designer handbags and pumps, who drive two luxury cars, who fund their 401(k)s, trade stocks, own a $500K+ home and likely a 2nd home, vacation regularly, earn $100K+ who are charged with finding a permanent solution for our homelessness crisis, NEVER will!

    ■ Their thinking is askew. How 'bout this.  If the shot callers would go live on the streets for a month, they'd have invaluable insight and revelations!

     ■ Walk in the shoes of the hopeless homeless for a month! Get belligerent for a month. Don't obey any law, enjoy sitting in the back of police cars, bunk behind cold stark bars, poop in the open, stink to high heaven, live in filth, wallow in stink, damp garbage, you call home.

     ■ Move all of your worldly belongings in your confiscated shopping cart, in the freezing cold, every goddamn day!

     ■ Shot callers are blinded by ignorance by not taking into account the realities of this horrific mental illness & addictions affecting and creating our homelessness. What is being allowed by governments (city, county, state) is absolutely inhumane, abusive and tragically preventable, long before the crisis level status pegged the meter.

     ■ A traveling hobo was sort of cool, in my day. More than one kid in my Coos Bay, Burns, Klamath Falls neighborhoods dressed as a hobo every Halloween! A hobo was even portrayed on "Leave it to Beaver."  That rascal (the Beav) in his naiveté even let the stinkin' drunk bum take a bubble bath in Ward & June Celeaver's tub.

     ● Can you imagine?

     ● Nobody chooses to live in filth and garbage!?

    ■  Decision makers are perhaps well-meaning but insincere, and completely incapable of resolving this human tradegy.

     ■ When the public-at-large see a homeless person sitting on the ground in filthy rags, or on the steps of the post office, they look right through them. They don't see a human being in grave despair.

   ■ Roughly 20 years ago, I had Logan Design make twenty hand-held signs (as seen at attachment to this email), that I use for Fairy Hearts Org, the charitable 🪽 "wing" 🪽 of Stevie Tenderheart Books, for the men and women (also known as) homeless hobos, to hold, instead of their torn pieces of cardboard with a black Sharpie message scribbled, asking for help.

     ■ When the public-at-large see a homeless person sitting on the ground in filthy rags, or on the steps of the post office, they look right through them. They don't see a human being in grave despair.

   ■ Roughly 20 years ago, I had Logan Design make twenty hand-held signs (as seen at attachment to this email), that I use for Fairy Hearts Org, the charitable 🪽 "wing" 🪽 of Stevie Tenderheart Books, for the men and women (also known as) homeless hobos, to hold, instead of their torn pieces of cardboard with a black Sharpie message scribbled, asking for help.

     ● The bright, clean, blue and white signage, clearly readable with Stevie's heartfelt message, "A Change of Heart Changes Everthing" actually got them seen!  Homeless people all over town were seen, perhaps for the first time. They made enough money to eat, and yes, buy their addictions.

     ● So while I was also well-meaning, I too was ignorant (naive) to the real issue at hand.

    ■ The ONLY real solution is to accept it.

     ■ People choose to be broken. They insist on living on the outside of society. Even with handouts, they are incapable and unwilling of leading a "normalized" existence.

     ■ By accepting this fact, this reality, only then can you truly help. It's wholly unrealistic to believe you can "fix" this segment of our population.

     ■ The best you can do is protect and serve the other segment. Those of us who VOTE, pay taxes, live within the lines of society. Who buy insurance, aren't addicted, have & hold jobs, share intelligence, ambition, follow laws, are socially active, and functioning, etc.

    ■ The best "WE" can do is create another society within a society. Camp Tenderheart, for a better name, is how, as detailed in my initial email.

    ●》The BEST "WE" can do is remove one segment of society from the other!

     ●》The BEST "WE" can do is come to terms with realities and that you cannot fix everything.

    ●》  The BEST "WE" can do is stop throwing taxpayer money away.

    ●》 The BEST "WE"  can do is think outside of the box!

     ●》The BEST "WE" can do is separate the aforementioned populations. They cannot and will not ever coexist! EVER!

     ●》The BEST "WE" can do is to stop trying to merge two diametrically opposites. The State Legislature is not the holy grail. Their political conflicts of interests and special interests prevent arriving at true solutions.

    ■ Give me your funding and I will remove this issue from your plate, forever. If not me, then someone with vision and the ability to implement practical applications for what is required.

     ■ You must incentivize the homeless.

     ●● CAMP TENDERHEART ...

     ●●● Build it and they WILL come!

    ■ There, I've added my two cents, again, to my previous 2 cents. And I still don't have a nickel.

🚨🚨POLICE ENDORSED!🚨🚨
I am honored to receive the endorsement of the Kingman Police Officer's Association (KPOA) for my re-election as Mohave County Supervisor of District 1. KPOA’s support is a testament to our shared commitment to law enforcement and public safety. Throughout my tenure on the Kingman City Council and now as County District 1 Supervisor, I have worked hard to support our police officers, Sheriff’s deputies, and detention officers to provide them the resources and tools they need to protect our community and our county.

Public safety is a cornerstone of our rural quality of life, and I am dedicated to supporting our police departments and MCSO in their collective efforts to make Mohave County a safer place for all its residents. Additionally, my work at the state level to secure our water supply since 2017 underscores my commitment to the long-term sustainability and well-being of our rural communities and county.

I am proud of what we have accomplished together and look forward to continuing this important work together. Thank you to KPOA for your trust and confidence. Together, we will keep Mohave County strong and secure. 🇺🇸🇺🇸🇺🇸

www.Travis4AZ.com

#SupportLawEnforcement #MohaveCountyStrong #PublicSafetyFirst

When Kim Konikow set out to find her forever home, her search ended in Fergus Falls — a community that "just felt right." Drawn by its vibrant arts scene and welcoming spirit, Kim now shares her own cultural traditions with her new neighbors. 

Through events like “Latkes and Hanukkah Traditions,” she invites the community to connect, cook, and celebrate together. “Sharing our cultures lessens the fear of the unknown,” she says. “It helps us be more welcoming.”

Building Hope, Building Community.

Our Hope Builders are a dedicated group of compassionate supporters of Habitat St. Johns, like you, who are making a tangible difference in the lives of families in St. Johns County every month.

As a Hope Builder, your ongoing, automatic monthly contribution provides a reliable source of funding that allows Habitat St. Johns to plan long-term projects, respond to urgent needs, and budget more effectively, thereby strengthening your impact.

This consistent support is crucial for creating sustainable, life-changing outcomes—helping families move from instability to security and from uncertainty to hope. By becoming a Hope Builder, you’re not just donating; you’re investing in the future of these families.

Join our Hope Builders at https://habitatstjohns.charityproud.org/GiveMonthly.

Thank you, for all you do to help us make a difference in our community!

text: The impact of Detroit politics on American politics, focusing on the upcoming August primary election featuring a Hollywood star. Hill Harper, who gained insights into community leadership and the Black power base in Detroit while studying at Harvard Law, has decided to run for office. Inspired by legends like Congressman Conyers, Congresswoman Carolyn Cheeks Kilpatrick, and Congressman Charles Diggs, Harper learned about their significant contributions to the city from two classmates. text:
(ok I sound tired but it was almost midnight! 😴)

Housing is a human right, and I believe we have a shared responsibility to allow more of it to address this crisis.

Last week, City Council discussed Culver City’s proposed zoning changes for the General Plan. And there's a lot worth celebrating— the proposal makes many single use neighborhoods across the city into mixed use.

That means more housing near jobs, amenities, and local businesses! 🏪🏘️🚍

But the plan also adds most of our new housing capacity to our densest and most diverse neighborhood (Fox Hills) which has less transit and fewer local businesses in walking distance, while leaving several other neighborhoods untouched. 

Allowing for more housing is a shared responsibility, and we all have a role to play to keep Culver City a diverse and inclusive place for all!

text:
Texas isn’t slowing down. We’re building smarter.

Recent legislation may have made things more complex—but not less profitable. Developers across oil & gas, renewables, transmission, fiber, and data center infrastructure are still pouring into Texas. Why? Because demand here isn’t theoretical. It’s real. It’s growing. And it’s urgent.

At Onward, our AI-trained Landmen, ROW agents, and Title teams are helping clients move faster across every type of linear project—from solar interconnects to pipeline corridors to redundant fiber feeds for AI data hubs.

We’re not choosing between energy sources. We’re building the infrastructure that connects them all.
 • Renewables to bridge the gap
 • Gas and pipelines to anchor the load
 • Transmission and fiber to tie it all together

This is how Texas stays on top—and we’re proud to be on the ground helping make it happen.

Your Goals. Our Mission. Onward.

text:
🚨 The Supreme Court Might Bring Back CONVERSION THERAPY?! 🚨

The harmful, debunked practice that falsely claims it can change someone’s sexual orientation or gender identity is under threat of making a comeback because the Supreme Court just agreed to hear a case that could overturn state bans.

📢 Every major mental health organization has condemned conversion therapy as junk science that leads to higher rates of depression, anxiety, and even suicidal ideation.

With a Master’s in Psychology specializing in gender & sexual fluidity and graduate certificate in marriage and family therapy, Kyle can tell you firsthand: this is NOT therapy. This is harm. Why would we bring back something that has been PROVEN to hurt people? 

Sound off in the comments 👇 

#lgbtq #conversiontherapy  #TransRightsAreHumanRights #transrights #equality

text:
The education challenges facing Africa are significant, but so is the continent's potential. Reports show that millions of children lack equitable access to quality education, with the number of out-of-school children still on the rise. With low foundational learning outcomes and a massive funding gap, there is an urgent need for collective action to unlock Africa's human potential.

At Chery Group, we believe education is the most powerful tool for sustainable change. We’re committed to moving beyond words—taking real action.

On August 21st, Chery Group will host the Champion for Education in Africa event, uniting governments, NGOs, and industry leaders to build partnerships and drive lasting impact for every child.

This is not just an event, but a commitment to a shared future. As Chery Group’s fastest-growing personalized brand, OMODA & JAECOO also upholds strong social responsibility, empowering change together.

#CheryGroupForAfrica #PoweringTomorrowThroughEducation #ChampionForEducationInAfrica #OMODAJAECOO

Agri-Pulse Newsmakers: Nov. 1, 2024 

Donald Trump won the presidency and the GOP won control of the Senate this week. Some House race votes are still being tallied.

We sat down with two former agriculture secretaries, Dan Glickman from the Clinton administration and Mike Johanns from the Bush administration, to discuss what could be ahead regarding trade and immigration policy, as well as the possible role of Robert F. Kennedy Jr. in the Trump administration.

text: THIS is what a strong sense of community is all about: coming together to support fellow Mainers in need. It takes all of us to ensure food insecurity doesn’t remain a top concern for Maine families. Through our county’s charitable arm, partnerships with the Good Shepherd Food Bank, local grocery stores & restaurants, and the generosity of community members, the York County Commission will ensure our regional food pantry will continue to operate and feed our neighbors.

text:
Over a century after its discovery, the Mount Lyell shrew has finally been photographed alive for the first time. Until last October, the Mount Lyell Shrew was the ONLY known mammal species in California that had never been photographed alive.

Last fall, California Creator for Nature Vishal Subramanyan visited the Eastern Sierra Nevada to study and document this animal. Despite this shrew being a species of special concern, scientists know next to nothing about it. They’re thought to be highly vulnerable to climate change impacts due to their dependence on high altitude habitats that are quickly disappearing.

To find this animal, Vishal and his colleagues worked around the clock, staying up through the night to maintain and monitor traps. They successfully found and photographed 5 individuals, and learned valuable ecological information about the species—and its neighbors! Careful planning, collaboration with UC Berkeley’s Museum of Vertebrate Zoology, and genetic testing at Cal Academy  helped confirm the identification of the species.
bert_topic_keywords: extinction, species, wildlife, petaluma, habitat, endangered, poaching, endangered species, animals, conservation

ad_creative_link_titles: Our Mental Health Matters,Our Mental Health Matters
ad_creative_link_descriptions:
Have you or a loved one struggled to find a therapist in Illinois who takes your insurance? Many of us are unable to find mental health care providers within our insurance networks because the insurance companies refuse to pay them fairly. We need mental health care, and our providers deserve to be compensated fairly for their expertise and skills.

The Improve Network Adequacy and Access for Behavioral Health Bill (HB1085) will require private insurers to pay therapists more adequately and make critical mental health care available to more Illinoisans. Send a letter to your legislator, urging them to pass this bill now!,Have you or a loved one struggled to find a therapist in Illinois who takes your insurance?
